machinery_genes.quote: A biochemical analysis was performed on three components: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin.
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier 2003 (Finlay lab), EPEC T3SS. EscC = SctC secretin. Also confirmed as the outer-ring component by Ogino 2006: 'EscC was a major protein component of the outer ring in the core basal body.'

machinery_genes.quote: The EscC outer ring protein was associated with both the EscF needle protein and EscD, a presumed inner membrane protein.
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006, EPEC T3SS apparatus assembly. EscD = SctD inner-membrane ring; escD mutant 'unable to produce the TTS apparatus' in the same paper.

machinery_genes.role: inner ring lipoprotein (connects inner and outer membrane rings)
machinery_genes.quote: EscF was also associated with EscJ, a presumed inner ring protein. Furthermore, escC, escD, and escJ mutant strains were unable to produce the TTS apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006, EPEC. EscJ = SctJ inner-ring lipoprotein.

machinery_genes.quote: Inner membrane-associated core (MA core) components include the EscRST family of proteins
machinery_genes.note: Gaytan/Wagner-style LEE specificity-switch review (J Bacteriol 2012). EscR = SctR export-gate component. Named as part of the EscRST inner-membrane export core of the LEE T3SS.

machinery_genes.quote: Inner membrane-associated core (MA core) components include the EscRST family of proteins
machinery_genes.note: LEE specificity-switch review 2012. EscS = SctS export-gate component, named within the EscRST core.

machinery_genes.quote: Inner membrane-associated core (MA core) components include the EscRST family of proteins
machinery_genes.note: LEE specificity-switch review 2012. EscT = SctT export-gate component, named within the EscRST core.

machinery_genes.quote: Secretion specificity is determined by the EscU family inner membrane core (M/C core) component.
machinery_genes.note: LEE specificity-switch review 2012. EscU = SctU autoprotease/switch. Independently confirmed as an interacting apparatus component (with EscP/EscI/EscF) in Monjaras Feria 2012 (PMID 22923600).

machinery_genes.quote: EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier 2003, EPEC. EscV = SctV major export-apparatus ring (homolog of LcrD/InvA/MxiA). Full clause: 'EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase; EscV, a predicted inner membrane protein; and EscC, a predicted outer membrane secretin.'

machinery_genes.quote: EscN, a predicted cytoplasmic ATPase
machinery_genes.note: Gauthier 2003, EPEC. EscN = SctN T3SS ATPase. Enzymatic activity confirmed in Romo-Castillo 2014 ('the ATPase EscN').

machinery_genes.quote: we investigated the role of the LEE-encoded EscO protein (previously Orf15 or EscA) in T3SS biogenesis. We show that EscO shares similar properties with the flagellar FliJ and the Yersinia YscO protein families.
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo 2014, EPEC injectisome. EscO = SctO ATPase stalk; interacts with EscN and EscL. (Note: protein previously called EscA/Orf15; see EscA entry for the distinct Orf15 vs Orf4 naming history.)

machinery_genes.quote: it was found to interact with the ATPase EscN and its negative regulator, EscL, of the export apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo 2014, EPEC. EscL = SctL stator/ATPase regulator. Also named essential for T3S in the EscL/EscN/EscQ literature.

machinery_genes.quote: we identified an interaction between EscK and the C-ring/sorting platform component EscQ
machinery_genes.note: Soto 2016, EPEC injectisome. EscQ = SctQ C-ring/sorting-platform protein.

machinery_genes.quote: EscK is crucial for efficient T3S and belongs to the SctK (OrgA/YscK/MxiK) protein family... EscK forms a protein complex with EscQ, the main component of the cytoplasmic sorting platform, serving as a docking site for T3S substrates.
machinery_genes.note: Soto 2016, EPEC. EscK (previously Orf4) = SctK sorting-platform base.

machinery_genes.quote: Orf16 is a T3SS-secreted substrate and regulates the secretion of the inner rod component EscI
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria/EscP paper 2012, EPEC. EscI = SctI inner-rod subunit; homologous to the EscF needle.

machinery_genes.quote: The EscC outer ring protein was associated with both the EscF needle protein and EscD
machinery_genes.note: Ogino 2006, EPEC. EscF = SctF needle subunit. Also named as 'the needle protein EscF' in Monjaras Feria 2012.

machinery_genes.quote: we propose that this protein be renamed EscP... Orf16 acts as a molecular measuring device in a way similar to that of members of the Yersinia YscP and flagellar FliK protein family.
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria 2012, EPEC. EscP (Orf16) = SctP molecular ruler; regulates needle length and EscI secretion. Not strictly essential but a bona fide apparatus component.

machinery_genes.role: periplasmic/inner-membrane structural protein (interacts with EscC secretin)
machinery_genes.quote: Orf15 was found to interact with the EPEC T3SS outer membrane ring component, EscC... we propose to rename it EscA.
machinery_genes.note: Cordes/Gonzalez-Pedrajo lab 2012, EPEC. Small LEE-encoded structural T3SS protein (Orf15) renamed EscA in this paper. NAMING-CONFLICT CAUTION: this same Orf15 was subsequently renamed EscO (Romo-Castillo 2014, PMID 24706741), which is the now-standard name (SctO stalk). Listed here as a single apparatus protein under both its historical names; do NOT double-count EscA and EscO as two separate components. Lower-confidence as a distinct entry; kept for completeness of the naming record. The Sct-unified name for this protein is SctO (see EscO entry).

machinery_genes.quote: These pathogens use the T3SS to sequentially secrete three categories of proteins: the T3SS needle and inner rod protein components; the EspA, EspB, and EspD translocators; and many LEE- and non-LEE-encoded effectors.
machinery_genes.note: Deng 2015, EPEC/EHEC. EspA forms the hollow sheath-like filament extending the needle (Ogino 2006: 'expandable EspA sheath-like structure'). Apparatus, not effector.

machinery_genes.quote: the EspA, EspB, and EspD translocators
machinery_genes.note: Deng 2015, EPEC/EHEC. EspB = translocon pore protein (apparatus, despite being secreted). Per the apparatus brief, translocon proteins are machinery.

machinery_genes.quote: the EspA, EspB, and EspD translocators
machinery_genes.note: Deng 2015, EPEC/EHEC. EspD = translocon pore protein. Apparatus per brief (forms host-membrane pore).

machinery_genes.quote: SepL has a unique role in type III secretion as a functional component of the translocation system that interacts with an essential element of the secretion machinery.
machinery_genes.note: O'Connell 2004 (Donnenberg/Kaper labs), EPEC. SepL = SctW gatekeeper (YopN/TyeA/InvE/MxiC family); required for translocator secretion. Apparatus, not a regulator-of-transcription.

machinery_genes.role: secretion component; SepL partner controlling translocator/effector hierarchy
machinery_genes.quote: SepL interacts with the LEE-encoded protein SepD. In contrast to SepL, we found that SepD is required for type III secretion of both translocation and effector proteins.
machinery_genes.note: O'Connell 2004, EPEC. SepD = secretion-machinery component forming the SepL-SepD gatekeeper complex. Essential for all type III secretion in this strain.

notes: EPEC E2348/69 has a SINGLE T3SS injectisome, encoded by the chromosomal LEE (locus of enterocyte effacement) pathogenicity island; this is the archetypal Esc/Sep injectisome of attaching/effacing pathogens. The Iguchi 2008 complete-genome paper (PMID 18952797) states E2348/69 'contains only 21 intact T3SS effector genes, all of which are carried on prophages and integrative elements' and that the ETT2-related second T3SS is ABSENT in this phylogroup-B2 strain. Therefore the instance label 'prophage_PP3' marks the genomic LOCATION of this instance's curated effectors (E2348C_0985/0986/0988), NOT a separate apparatus: those non-LEE effectors are translocated through the one LEE-encoded injectisome. This is the separated-paper / shared-apparatus case (curation rule 4): the answer-key machinery is the LEE Esc/Sep apparatus, characterized in EPEC E2348/69 itself (Elliott 1998 complete LEE sequence, GenBank AF022236) and in follow-up molecular studies of the same strain/system (Gauthier 2003, O'Connell 2004, Ogino 2006, the 2012 J Bacteriol injectisome series, Romo-Castillo 2014, Deng 2015). Core apparatus covered gene-by-gene with verbatim EPEC quotes: secretin EscC; basal-body rings EscD, EscJ; export apparatus EscR/EscS/EscT/EscU/EscV; ATPase complex EscN/EscO/EscL; sorting platform/C-ring EscQ/EscK; inner rod EscI; needle EscF; ruler EscP; needle-filament sheath EspA; translocon pore EspB/EspD; gatekeeper SepL/SepD. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted effectors incl. this instance's E2348C_0985/0986/0988 and the LEE effectors Tir, EspF, EspG, EspH, EspZ, Map; the intimin adhesin Eae and its receptor Tir; effector chaperones CesT, CesAB, CesD, CesD2, CesF; and transcriptional regulators Ler, GrlA, GrlR. LOW-CONFIDENCE / CAVEAT CALLS: (1) EscA and EscO are the SAME LEE protein (Orf15), renamed twice (EscA in PMID 22447907, then EscO in PMID 24706741); both names are listed for the naming record but represent ONE apparatus component (SctO) and must not be double-counted, so the true distinct-component count is one fewer than the row count. (2) EscR/EscS/EscT are quoted collectively as the 'EscRST family' from the 2012 review rather than each by an individual functional paper; their existence as the export-gate triad in the LEE is not in doubt. (3) EscP is a needle-length ruler that is not strictly essential but is a bona fide structural apparatus member. EtgA (a LEE-encoded T3SS-specialized peptidoglycan lytic enzyme) and the pilotin/InvH-type secretin chaperone were noted in the literature but not added as separate rows without a clean EPEC naming quote in hand; EtgA could be added if the benchmark wants the muramidase counted. All quotes are verbatim from PubMed-indexed abstracts/records; every DOI and PMID was confirmed real via get_article_metadata.
